transcript: Hey, Dan! My name’s Jarvis. I just, ah—
—I’m new to the neighborhood here! What’s goin’ on with you? I got your number from Big Red over on the other side.
Fine! What can I help you with?

Uh, yeah, I’m new to the neighborhood, like I say. Name’s Jarvis Buzzer. So, yeah, I moved down from Normal about four days back. And I just got this new place and it—could—do you think there’s any way you could give me a hand, maybe, a little bit later? I just have this, ah, this sander; could you help me with it, just real fast? I can give you few dollars!
I’m sorry! I’m still not sure on who I’m talkin’ to.
It’s J.B. callin’. What do you got goin’ on?—
You’re just fiddlin’ around ‘cause you don’t wanna help me. I offered ya five, ten dollars fair ’n square! [exhales] I just wanna hook up a pulley in my house, here; that’s all’s I need!
I’m Jarvis. I’m from Normal. I lived here about a week now, alright? So, where do we stand, here, tough guy? [delay]
So, does—did Justin have you do this?
Justin. A—ya—are you related to him?
My brother helped me move here; is that who you’re talkin’ about?
—I’m Jarvis Buzzer. Moved down from Normal.
So this is—do you, like, record this and put it on the internet? Or you record it so he can listen to it?

No, I—I need help at my house, here. Big Red said that you were a handy kinda guy and that you might help me. I guess he was wrong, though, because you don’t seem that willing to help me out. I’m new to the area here! Ya understand? [delay] Because that’s what I’m tryin’ ta—need your help with—
—Well that’s why I—that’s why I thought this was related Justin. Because he kinda talks like that.
I don’t know who that is.
Oh. Well that’s who I think it is.
Well, you’re dead wrong! How’s that make ya feel? [delay]—

—You think you’re right about which part? [sfx] That I’ll come over and whoop ya in the aaa?! [delay] ‘Cause, buddy, I’ll come over there and I’ll kick y’aaa! [exhales] [delay] Ya feel me? I talked to everybody on the street; I talked to Ernie, I talked to Gustavo, I frickin’ talked to Octavio up at the grocery store. They all said, “Oh, yeah! Call Dan up!” And all you’ve given me is a lump o’ coal, here, today! [exhales]
And I don’t like what I hear! [delay] I don’t like what I’m hearin’ up here! [sfx]
Why don’t you tell me who you really are?
Jarvis! Jarvis! Jarvis! For the eighteenth time! Born 'n reared!—
—Who don’t you tell me you—who you really are? Who you really are?—

—I come down here and then I got some neighbor of mine trottin’ around like he’s to cool for school—doesn’t believe a word I say! He wants to get buckwild. Well buddy, let’s get buckwile! I’ll twirl you aroun’! Ya got me figured out? I offered ya some money—
—fair 'n square to help me hook up m’pulley here! And now you wanna get buckwile? [delay] Buddy, let’s get buckwile!
I offered ya the money fair 'n square to help me with my pulley! And you don’t believe where I’m from—
—you don’t believe my names? What do you want from me?
W—let’s start with this: tell me your real name.
I’m not gonna say it again—
I’ve told ya 20 times who I am! And where I got your number! I’m new to the community over here!
‘Kay. [inhales] So, obviously, I don’t believe that. So why do you keep sayin’ it?
So why not? Big 'n Bad? What’s your real name?

[scoffs] I thought it was “Dan”! ‘Parenty you got everything figured out—you’re so much smarter than me—so, okay. What do I need to know around here, if nothing is as it seems, hm? [inhales] [exhales]
I—this is what I would tell ya: If you don’t have anything to do with Justin, um, you talk just like him.
The kinda guy that think this is funny and talks just like you.
Buddy, I’m 59 years of age! Born 'n reared in Normal, Illinois. And I just moved down here a week ago! Was lookin’ for a hand from my neighbor! ‘Cause that’s the kinda guy I am; I’m a neighbor—
—W—well I’m not gonna do it! I’m not gonna do it for you—
Huh? It would take ya all of walkin’ across the street; is that too much to ask up here, or what do you think? [delay]
Well like I said, gimme your address. I’ll walk over there; I said that yesterday—
—Oh, so now you want the money; is that it?
No! I just wanna meet you.
Oh, why is that? You don’t even believe a flippin’ word I say!
I like—well, maybe if I met ya in person…
Yeah! Maybe if I met ya in person I’ll kick y’aaa! [delay] [cracks up] [sfx]
Same street as you, Tough Guy!
I’ll come on over to your place! Are you there now? I’ll come over there now! Show ya how we do things in Normal!—
—No! I’ll—I’ll head right over. Just tell me the street name.
And then you’ll know my name!—Jarvis Buzzer—all day long!
What’s your s—what’s your street name, there—?
Six-Zero-Two-Two. What’s the street? Why can’t you answer that? What’s wrong with you? Say the street name.
I’m on Mocal Drive! D’you understand?—
—Prolly suppose you don’t believe that, either, do ya?
‘Cause you said it’s the street I live on! That’s not the street I live on!
Oh, it sure the hell is!
Listen, Big Red told me I should—
I wanna put up a towel holder in my new residence. But apparently, that’s—
Well, uh [waffles] I haven’t pulled the trigger yet on that one! [delay] ‘Kay? But I need a hand up here—
—put up some new face places for my power outlets. And I thought you could give me a hand!
Okay. Why—why did you think that?
Big Red said you were a helpful kinda guy. He had to go to work! Buddy, you’ve just given me nothin’ but a runaround up here! [delay]
So that’s why I say maybe I should just come over and, ah, [layered & pitch fx] just settle the score with ya here, one time! How ‘bout I come up and settle the score, big guy?—
[ LPC's audio is continually layered for a minute and a half]
—Alright, what would you wanna—what—what score to settle? What do you want?
I’m gonna level you with my bullwhip! [exhales]
Am I makin’ sense to you? Dan? [exhales]
‘Cause I’ll come up and kick ya ass!
Jarvis Buzzer. Put me into your search engine, if you don’t believe me!
Okay! I’ll plug it into my search engine! What would I find?
That I’m from Normal, Illinois. I just moved here about seven, ten days ago.
Why is it echoing like that?

Yeah! Why do you keep echoing? “What’s your name?” “What’s your name?” “What’s your name?” “What’s your name?” “What’s your name?” Why don’t you just get in gear, guy? Get with the program already! Huh? I’m new to the community over here!—
—This is obviously usin’ a lotta software, voice manipulation—
—and somethin’. So this is, like, a prank call app?—
—Do you have a computer rig that does this or what?
Oh, okay! Well why don’t you take a break—get off your rear end—and come help me like a real man? With my towel holder!
Tu—turn off the reverb, bro. Take—take off the reverb. You’re gonna call every day?
—No, you’re gonna come over—help me with my pulley and my sander like I originally asked!—‘cause it’s a two-man job. And then we’ll—we’ll call it good. I’ll leave you be!
